note_data.title: Poetry — Part II
keyPoints: Poetry is the expression of feelings and ideas through rhythmical composition of words
Rhyme scheme is the pattern of rhymes at the end of each line of poetry
Meter refers to the rhythmic structure of verse based on stressed and unstressed syllables
Alliteration is the repetition of initial consonant sounds in successive words
Metaphor is a figure of speech that makes an implicit comparison between two unlike things
Personification gives human characteristics to non-human objects or abstract ideas
Simile is a figure of speech that compares two different things using 'like' or 'as'
Theme is the central idea or underlying meaning of a poem
Imagery appeals to the senses through vivid descriptive language

definitions.definition: Poetry is the expression of feelings and ideas in language chosen and arranged to create a specific emotional response through meaning, sound and rhythm.
Rhyme scheme is the pattern of rhymes at the end of each line of poetry, usually marked with letters like ABAB or AABB.
Metaphor is a figure of speech in which a word or phrase is applied to an object or action to which it is not literally applicable.
Alliteration is the occurrence of the same letter or sound at the beginning of adjacent or closely connected words.
Personification is the attribution of human characteristics to something non-human or the representation of an abstract quality in human form.

importantQuestions.question: Define rhyme scheme with an example.
importantQuestions.answer: Rhyme scheme is the pattern of rhymes at the end of each line of poetry. For example, in ABAB rhyme scheme, the first and third lines rhyme, and the second and fourth lines rhyme.

importantQuestions.question: What are the main figures of speech used in poetry? Explain with examples.
importantQuestions.answer: Main figures of speech include: 1) Metaphor - direct comparison without using 'like' or 'as' (Life is a journey), 2) Simile - comparison using 'like' or 'as' (brave as a lion), 3) Personification - giving human qualities to non-human things (The wind whispered), 4) Alliteration - repetition of initial sounds (Peter Piper picked), 5) Hyperbole - deliberate exaggeration (I've told you a million times). These devices enhance the beauty and meaning of poetry.

mcqs.question: Which figure of speech is used in 'The stars danced in the sky'?
What is the rhyme scheme of a quatrain where first and third lines rhyme, second and fourth lines rhyme?
'She sells sea shells by the sea shore' is an example of:

commonMistakes: Students often confuse metaphor with simile - remember metaphor doesn't use 'like' or 'as'
Mixing up rhyme scheme patterns - always mark with letters systematically
Forgetting to give examples when defining figures of speech
Not identifying the correct literary device in given verses
Writing incomplete definitions without mentioning key characteristics
examTips: Poetry questions usually carry 8-10 marks in BSEK papers
Always give examples when explaining figures of speech
Learn to identify rhyme schemes quickly by marking end words
Memorize exact definitions as they appear in STBB textbook
Practice identifying literary devices in given poetry excerpts
